床.origin: A roof 广 sheltering wood 木 — a wooden frame under a house where you sleep: a bed.

sentence.en: I get up at seven every morning.

疑.origin: Picture someone stopping at a crossroads, unsure which way to go — that puzzled, head-scratching feeling is doubt.

sentence.en: I have a little question about this problem.

举.origin: Lots of hands working together to lift something up high — 举 means to raise.

sentence.en: If you have a question, please raise your hand.

低.origin: A person 亻 bending way down toward the ground — they make themselves low.

sentence.en: The little bird flies very low.

乡.origin: The flowing lines look like winding country paths and rivers leading home to your village.

sentence.en: I love my hometown.

楼.origin: Wood 木 stacked up floor by floor — that's how you build a tall, many-story building.

sentence.en: My home is on the third floor.

层.origin: Under a roof 尸, things pile up in layers — 层 is a floor or layer.

sentence.en: This building has ten floors.

造.origin: The walking radical 辶 plus 告 — you go to work and tell people what to make: you build it.

sentence.en: The workers are building a house.

代.origin: A person 亻 standing in for another as time marches on — one generation, one era.

sentence.en: I will go to the meeting on your behalf.

竹.origin: Two tall stalks with leaves hanging down — a perfect picture of bamboo.

sentence.en: Pandas love to eat bamboo the most.

试.origin: Speech 讠 next to 式 (a method) — you talk through a way of doing something: you try it.

sentence.en: I want to give it a try.

验.origin: A horse 马 plus 佥 — long ago people checked a horse carefully before buying it: to test and inspect.

sentence.en: We did an experiment.

皮.origin: A hand using a tool to peel the hide off — 皮 means skin or peel.

sentence.en: Don't throw the banana peel around.

破.origin: A rock 石 hitting skin 皮 — something cracks and tears: it breaks.

sentence.en: My cup is broken.

布.origin: A hand spreading out a piece of cloth 巾 — 布 is cloth or fabric.

sentence.cn: 妈妈用布做了一个袋子。
sentence.en: Mom made a bag out of cloth.

术.origin: Wood 木 with a small mark — think of a clever skill grown like a branch: 术 means skill or art.

sentence.en: I like art class.

之.origin: Shaped like a little foot stepping forward — an old word that means to go or links words together.

sentence.en: This is the book I had before.

砍.origin: A rock 石 swung with an open mouth 欠 of effort — you chop and hack with it.

sentence.en: He chops wood with an axe.

建.origin: A hand holding a brush 聿 plus the walking line 廴 — drawing plans and stepping out to build.

sentence.en: The workers are building a bridge.

房.origin: A door 户 with 方 below it — step through the door into a room: a house or room.

sentence.en: My room is very clean.

粗.origin: Grain 米 next to 且 — big, chunky grains feel thick and rough: 粗 means thick or coarse.

sentence.en: This tree is very thick.

累.origin: A field 田 piled over silk threads 糸 — heavy work stacking up makes you tired.

sentence.en: After walking all day I am very tired.

路.origin: A foot 足 beside 各 — feet going their separate ways trace out a road.

sentence.en: Be careful when crossing the road.

难.origin: A hand 又 trying to catch a flying bird 隹 — so tricky to grab! That's why 难 means difficult.

sentence.en: This problem is too hard.

抓.origin: A hand 扌 with claws 爪 reaching out — grab and hold on tight!

sentence.en: The kitten grabbed the ball of yarn.

疼.origin: The sickness sign 疒 with 冬 (winter) inside — a cold, sore ache: 疼 means it hurts.

sentence.en: My tummy hurts a little.

痛.origin: The sickness sign 疒 wrapped around 甬 — that sharp, painful feeling: 痛 means pain.

sentence.en: His tooth hurts a lot.

血.origin: A drop 丿 falling into a bowl 皿 — it pictures a bowl of blood.

sentence.en: His hand is bleeding.

棵.origin: Tree wood 木 next to fruit 果 — 棵 is the counting word we use for trees.

sentence.en: There is a big tree by the door.

利.origin: A blade 刂 cutting ripe grain 禾 — a sharp tool brings a good harvest and benefit.

sentence.en: This knife is very sharp.

细.origin: Silk 纟 beside a tiny field 田 — silk threads are super thin: 细 means fine or slender.

sentence.en: Please look carefully.

如.origin: A woman 女 next to a mouth 口 — listening and going along, meaning 'as' or 'like'.

sentence.en: If it rains, don't go out.

切.origin: The number 七 next to a knife 刀 — bring the knife down and slice it: 切 means to cut.

sentence.en: Mom is cutting fruit.

逛.origin: The walking radical 辶 with 狂 — wandering around freely with no rush: to stroll.

sentence.en: On the weekend we stroll in the park.

力.origin: Shaped like a strong plow or a flexed arm pushing hard — 力 means strength and power.

sentence.en: He is really strong.

暖.origin: The sun 日 next to 爰 — sunshine spreading over you makes everything cozy and warm.

sentence.en: The weather in spring is warm.

曲.origin: Picture a bent, folded shape with lines turning inside — 曲 means crooked or curved.

sentence.en: This little path twists and turns.

哪.origin: A mouth 口 next to 那 — the mouth asks the question 'which one?' or 'where?'

sentence.en: Where do you live?

款.origin: Think of 欠 (owing) on the side — money you give or owe: 款 means funds or a payment.

sentence.en: This kind of toy is really fun.

借.origin: A person 亻 next to 昔 — one person hands something to another for a while: to borrow or lend.

sentence.en: May I borrow your pen?
